questions.question: You've been in a relationship for a while, but lately, you've been feeling unfulfilled and disconnected from your partner. You can't seem to muster up the enthusiasm for the things you used to enjoy together, and you find yourself wondering if you're just going through the motions. You've been thinking about the reasons why you started dating your partner in the first place, and you realize that those reasons don't seem as important to you anymore. You're torn between your commitment to your partner and your growing feelings of disconnection. You know that breaking up would be difficult for both of you, but you're not sure if staying in the relationship is the right decision for you. What would you do?
options: End the relationship immediately, without discussing it with your partner, to avoid prolonging the inevitable.
Have an open and honest conversation with your partner about your feelings and concerns, and work together to decide the best course of action.
Try to rekindle the spark in the relationship by planning romantic getaways, surprise dates, and other activities that might help you reconnect with your partner.
Stay in the relationship out of obligation or convenience, even if it means sacrificing your own happiness.
questions.explanation: Have an open and honest conversation with your partner about your feelings and concerns, and work together to decide the best course of action. Reflect on your feelings: Take time to understand why you're feeling disconnected from your partner and what you hope to achieve in the relationship. Communicate with your partner: Have an open and honest conversation with your partner about your feelings and concerns, and listen to their perspective as well. Evaluate the relationship: Consider the pros and cons of staying in the relationship and whether it's truly fulfilling for both partners. Make a decision together: Work together to decide the best course of action, whether it's to work on the relationship or to part ways.

questions.question: In this question, a statement is followed by three courses of action, numbered I, II and III. You must assume everything in the statement to be true and on the basis of the information given in the statement, decide which course/s of action logically follows for pursuing. Statement: The local residents have complained that the newly constructed flyover has increased noise pollution in the area. Courses of action: I. The flyover should be demolished immediately. II. Noise-reducing measures should be implemented on the flyover. III. The residents should be relocated to a different area.
options: Only course of action I follow.
Only course of action II follows.
Both courses of action I and II follow.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is II. Noise-reducing measures should be implemented on the flyover. This is because the statement highlights a specific issue [noise pollution] that can be addressed through targeted measures [noise-reducing measures]. Demolishing the flyover [I] is an extreme measure, and relocating the residents [III] does not address the root issue.

You're walking down a busy street on a sunny afternoon when you hear a loud crash followed by the sound of a child crying. You turn around to see a young boy, probably around 8-10 years old, lying on the ground next to his bicycle. He appears to have fallen off his bike and scraped his knee. The child is visibly shaken and crying, and his knee is bleeding slightly. Several people are passing by, but no one seems to have stopped to help. What do you do?
options: Ignore the child and continue walking.
Call the child's parents or guardians immediately.
Approach the child calmly, offer comfort, and assess the injury.
Scold the child for not being more careful.
questions.explanation: Approach the child calmly, offer comfort, and assess the injury. Approach calmly: Walk up to the child in a calm and gentle manner to avoid further distress. Offer comfort: Kneel down, offer a reassuring smile, and provide comfort to help the child feel safe and secure. Assess the injury: Gently examine the scraped knee to determine the severity of the injury and decide if further medical attention is needed. Provide basic first aid: If the injury is minor, offer basic first aid, such as cleaning the wound with water and applying a band-aid. By responding promptly and compassionately, you can help the child feel more comfortable and secure.

The suspension lift means that Indian wrestlers can now compete in upcoming tournaments under their country’s flag.

questions.question: During the tenure of which Viceroy, the Meerut conspiracy trial place?
questions.explanation: The Meerut Conspiracy Case was a controversial court case that took place during the tenure of Viceroy Lord Irwin [1926-1931]. In March 1929, the British government imprisoned 31 labor leaders and brought them to Meerut and put them on trial. Amongthem were three British communists, Philip Spratt, Bain Bradley and Lester Hutchinson. > मेरठ षडयंत्र केस एक विवादास्पद अदालती मामला था जो वायसराय लॉर्ड इरविन [1926-1931] के कार्यकाल के दौरान हुआ था। मार्च 1929 में, ब्रिटिश सरकार ने 31 मजदूर नेताओं को कैद कर लिया और उन्हें मेरठ लाकर उन पर मुकदमा चलाया। उनमें तीन ब्रिटिश कम्युनिस्ट, फिलिप स्प्रैट, बैन ब्रैडली और लेस्टर हचिंसन भी शामिल थे।

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Western Ghats in India: 1. The Western Ghats are continuous hills running parallel to the western coast of India and are older than the Himalayas. 2. They influence the southwest monsoon and cause heavy rainfall on their windward side while creating a rain shadow on the leeward side. 3. They are not rich in biodiversity and are primarily composed of igneous rock formations. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Western Ghats, also known as the Sahyadri Hills, are a continuous mountain range running parallel to India’s western coast and are geologically older than the Himalayas. They act as a climatic barrier by intercepting the southwest monsoon winds, resulting in heavy rainfall on the windward side (like Kerala and coastal Karnataka) and dry conditions on the leeward side (rain shadow areas like interior Maharashtra and Karnataka). These ranges are exceptionally rich in biodiversity and are classified as a UNESCO World Heritage site. Contrary to Statement 3, the Western Ghats are one of the eight hottest hotspots of biological diversity in the world. Hence, Statements 1 and 2 are correct, while Statement 3 is incorrect.

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding Article 13 of the Indian Constitution: 1. Article 13 declares that all laws in force in the territory of India before the commencement of the Constitution, insofar as they are inconsistent with the provisions of Part III, shall be void to the extent of such inconsistency. 2. It empowers the Parliament to amend Fundamental Rights under Article 368 without any limitation. 3. Judicial review of pre-constitutional and post-constitutional laws is derived from Article 13. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: Statement 1 is correct: Article 13(1) makes pre-constitutional laws void if inconsistent with Fundamental Rights. Statement 2 is incorrect: In Kesavananda Bharati, it was ruled that Parliament cannot abrogate Fundamental Rights beyond the "basic structure". Statement 3 is correct: Article 13 lays the foundation for judicial review in India.

questions.question: What is the significance of wetlands in mitigating natural disasters?
options: They exacerbate floods and droughts>वे बाढ़ और सूखे को बढ़ाते है
They act as natural buffers against calamities>वे आपदाओं के खिलाफ प्राकृतिक बफर के रूप में कार्य करते हैं
They accelerate soil erosion >वे मिट्टी के कटाव को तेज करते है
They deplete groundwater resources>वे भूजल संसाधनों को नष्ट करते है
questions.explanation: Wetlands serve as natural buffers against floods, droughts, and other calamities, safeguarding communities and infrastructure. > समाधान: आर्द्रभूमि बाढ़, सूखे और अन्य आपदाओं के खिलाफ प्राकृतिक अवरोधक के रूप में काम करती हैं तथा समुदायों और बुनियादी ढांचे की सुरक्षा करती हैं।

questions.question: Which tropical deciduous plant is commonly found on the Deccan Plateau?
questions.explanation: Sandalwood, a tropical deciduous tree which lies in region of rainfall between 100 cm to 200 cm. It is a class of woods from trees in the genus Santalum. Unlike many other aromatic woods, these woods are heavy yellow and fine grained. The Deccan area lies in the region receiving between 100 cm to 200 cm rainfall. > चंदन, एक उष्णकटिबंधीय पर्णपाती वृक्ष है जो 100 सेमी से 200 सेमी के बीच वर्षा वाले क्षेत्र में पाया जाता है। यह सैंटलम वंश के पेड़ों की एक श्रेणी है। कई अन्य सुगंधित लकड़ियों के विपरीत, ये लकड़ियाँ गहरे पीले रंग की और महीन दाने वाली होती हैं। दक्कन क्षेत्र 100 सेमी से 200 सेमी के बीच वर्षा वाले क्षेत्र में स्थित है।

questions.question: Who were the first among the following to establish trade relations with India?
questions.explanation: Portuguese were the first Europeans to start trade with India. After the fall of Ottoman Empire and capture of Constantinople in 1453 it became difficult for Europeans to trade with India via land route. So they searched for new sea route and in 1498 Vasco da Gama of Portugal discovered new sea route via Cape of Good Hope to India > पुर्तगाली भारत के साथ व्यापार शुरू करने वाले पहले यूरोपीय थे। 1453 में ओटोमन साम्राज्य के पतन और कॉन्स्टेंटिनोपल पर कब्ज़ा करने के बाद यूरोपीय लोगों के लिए भारत के साथ ज़मीनी रास्ते से व्यापार करना मुश्किल हो गया। इसलिए उन्होंने नए समुद्री मार्ग की खोज की और 1498 में पुर्तगाल के वास्को डी गामा ने केप ऑफ़ गुड होप के ज़रिए भारत तक पहुँचने के लिए नया समुद्री मार्ग खोज निकाला।

questions.question: Which Indian writer was awarded with the ‘Erasmus Prize’ 2024 for highlighting the climate change crisis?
questions.explanation: Indian writer Amitav Ghosh, aged 67, has been awarded the prestigious ‘Erasmus Prize’ in 2024 by the Premium Erasmianum Foundation of the Netherlands. Recognized for his passionate contributions in highlighting the global climate change crisis through literature, Ghosh will receive the award, with an incentive of 150,000 Euros, in person in November. The award, established in 1958, honors individuals or institutions making exceptional contributions to culture or scholarship in Europe and beyond, emphasizing the cultural dimensions of the climate crisis. > भारतीय लेखक अमिताव घोष, जिनकी आयु 67 वर्ष है, को नीदरलैंड के प्रीमियम इरास्मियनम फाउंडेशन द्वारा 2024 में प्रतिष्ठित 'इरास्मस पुरस्कार' से सम्मानित किया गया है। साहित्य के माध्यम से वैश्विक जलवायु परिवर्तन संकट को उजागर करने में उनके भावुक योगदान के लिए पहचाने जाने वाले घोष नवंबर में व्यक्तिगत रूप से 150,000 यूरो के प्रोत्साहन के साथ पुरस्कार प्राप्त करेंगे। 1958 में स्थापित यह पुरस्कार, जलवायु संकट के सांस्कृतिक आयामों पर जोर देते हुए यूरोप और उसके बाहर संस्कृति या विद्वत्ता में असाधारण योगदान देने वाले व्यक्तियों या संस्थानों को सम्मानित करता है।

questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding acids and bases: 1. According to Arrhenius theory, acids are substances that increase the concentration of H⁺ ions in aqueous solution. 2. According to Brønsted-Lowry theory, a base is a proton donor and an acid is a proton acceptor. 3. A strong acid completely dissociates in water, while a weak acid does so only partially. Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.explanation: The Arrhenius theory correctly defines acids as substances that release H⁺ ions in water. A strong acid (like HCl) dissociates completely, while a weak acid (like CH₃COOH) does not. However, in Brønsted-Lowry theory, the definitions are reversed—acids are proton donors, and bases are proton acceptors, so Statement 2 is incorrect.

questions.question: By which of the following amendments was it made the fundamental duty of parents to provide education to their children in the age group of 6 to 14 years?
questions.explanation: By the 86th Constitutional Amendment Act 2002 11th fundamental duty was added. This ensured that education opportunities were provided to our children in the age group of 6 to 14 years. > 86वें संविधान संशोधन अधिनियम 2002 द्वारा 11वां मौलिक कर्तव्य जोड़ा गया। इससे यह सुनिश्चित हुआ कि 6 से 14 वर्ष की आयु के बच्चों को शिक्षा के अवसर उपलब्ध कराए जाएं।

questions.question: Which of the following puppet dance is performed in Orissa?
questions.explanation: Puppet dance Kandhei or Sakhi Kandhei is a string puppetry show popular in the Indian state of Odisha, especially in the Kendrapara district of Odisha. > कठपुतली नृत्य कंधेई या सखी कंधेई एक तार कठपुतली शो है जो भारतीय राज्य ओडिशा में विशेष रूप से ओडिशा के केंद्रपाड़ा जिले में लोकप्रिय है।

questions.question: The idea of Ombudsman has been taken from which of the following countries?
questions.explanation: The concept of an Ombudsman has been borrowed from Sweden in India. It was first used in 1809 when the Swedish parliament, established the office of Ombudsman to look after citizens interests in their dealings with government. Pinaki Chandra Ghose was the first and present Ombudsman/Lokpal of India. > भारत में लोकपाल की अवधारणा स्वीडन से ली गई है। इसका पहली बार इस्तेमाल 1809 में किया गया था जब स्वीडिश संसद ने सरकार के साथ अपने व्यवहार में नागरिकों के हितों की देखभाल के लिए लोकपाल का कार्यालय स्थापित किया था। पिनाकी चंद्र घोष भारत के पहले और वर्तमान लोकपाल थे।

questions.question: Which of the following statements is/are incorrect? 1. Mitochondria, often termed the "powerhouses of the cell," generate ATP through oxidative phosphorylation and possess their own circular DNA and 70S ribosome’s, similar to prokaryotic cells. 2. The Golgi apparatus is responsible for modifying, sorting, and packaging proteins and lipids received from the endoplasmic reticulum, and it plays a key role in Lysosomes formation. 3. Ribosome is membrane-bound organelles found only in the nucleus, and they synthesize proteins using mRNA templates through a process called transcription. 4. Lysosomes contain hydrolytic enzymes capable of digesting old cell organelles and foreign particles, and their rupture can lead to autolysis of the cell.
questions.explanation: Only Statement 3 is incorrect. Ribosomes are non-membranous organelles (they have no membrane). They are found freely in the cytoplasm or attached to the rough ER, not only in the nucleus. Ribosomes carry out translation, not transcription. Transcription: DNA → mRNA (in the nucleus) Translation: mRNA → Protein (by ribosomes in the cytoplasm)

questions.question: Which of the following is a group of bacteria found in human intestines, whose presence in water indicates contamination by diseasecausing micro-organisms?
questions.explanation: Coliform is a bacterial grouping which found in human's intestine. Its presence in water indicates disease causing micro-organism's contamination. It used as a microbiological indicator.

questions.question: What is the name given to the graph that shows all the combinations of two commodities that a consumer can afford at given market prices and within the particular income level in economic terms?
questions.explanation: Budget Line also known as budget constraint is a graphical delineation of all possible combination of the two commodities that can be bought with given income. Budget line shows the purchasing power and market value of both the products. Budget line slopes downward with negative slope show reverse correlation between two products. It is tangent to indifference curve. > बजट रेखा जिसे बजट बाधा के रूप में भी जाना जाता है, दो वस्तुओं के सभी संभावित संयोजनों का एक ग्राफिक चित्रण है, जिसे दी गई आय से खरीदा जा सकता है। बजट रेखा दोनों उत्पादों की क्रय शक्ति और बाजार मूल्य को दर्शाती है। बजट रेखा नकारात्मक ढलान के साथ नीचे की ओर झुकती है जो दो उत्पादों के बीच विपरीत सहसंबंध दिखाती है। यह उदासीनता वक्र की स्पर्श रेखा है।

questions.question: The chemical formula of Acetic acid is ………. . > एसिटिक एसिड का रासायनिक सूत्र ………. है।
questions.explanation: Acetic acid also known as ethanoic acid. It is an organic acid, whose chemical formula is CH3COOH. CH3COOH is the simplest carboxylic acid and also an important chemical reagent. Acetic acid is found predominately is vinegar. > एसिटिक एसिड को एथेनोइक एसिड के नाम से भी जाना जाता है। यह एक कार्बनिक अम्ल है, जिसका रासायनिक सूत्र CH3COOH है। CH3COOH सबसे सरल कार्बोक्जिलिक एसिड है और एक महत्वपूर्ण रासायनिक अभिकर्मक भी है। एसिटिक एसिड मुख्य रूप से सिरके में पाया जाता है।

questions.question: What was invented by 'Zacharias Janseen'?
questions.explanation: Zacharias Janssen invented the microscope in 1600. Zacharias Janssen invented the microscope in 1600. questions.question: Consider the following statements regarding the Repo Rate and Reverse Repo Rate in India: 1. Repo Rate is the rate at which the RBI lends short-term money to commercial banks. 2. Reverse Repo Rate is the rate at which RBI borrows money from banks. 3. An increase in Repo Rate encourages banks to lend more aggressively to customers. Which statements are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct, while Statement 3 is incorrect because when the Repo Rate increases, borrowing from the RBI becomes more expensive for banks. As a result, banks raise their lending rates, making loans costlier for customers. This reduces the demand for loans and slows down credit growth—serving as a tool to control inflation rather than to encourage lending.

questions.question: ‘DHARMA GUARDIAN’ exercise, recently seen in the news, is conducted between which two countries?
questions.explanation: The 5th edition of Joint Military Exercise ‘Dharma Guardian’ began at Mahajan Field Firing Ranges, Rajasthan, India, lasting from February 25 to March 9, 2024. It involves the Indian Army and Japan Ground Self-Defense Force, emphasizing joint tactical manoeuvres in a simulated semi-urban setting. The exercise aims to enhance interoperability and strengthen military cooperation, aligning with Chapter VII of the United Nations Charter. > संयुक्त सैन्य अभ्यास 'धर्म गार्जियन' का 5वां संस्करण महाजन फील्ड फायरिंग रेंज, राजस्थान, भारत में शुरू हुआ, जो 25 फरवरी से 9 मार्च, 2024 तक चलेगा। इसमें भारतीय सेना और जापान ग्राउंड सेल्फ डिफेंस फोर्स शामिल हैं, जो एक नकली अर्ध-शहरी सेटिंग में संयुक्त सामरिक युद्धाभ्यास पर जोर देते हैं। इस अभ्यास का उद्देश्य संयुक्त राष्ट्र चार्टर के अध्याय VII के साथ तालमेल बिठाते हुए अंतर-संचालन को बढ़ाना और सैन्य सहयोग को मजबूत करना है।

questions.question: You are a contestant on a game show and face ten doors. Behind one door is a bike (prize), and behind the other doors are dogs. You choose door number 1. The host, who knows what's behind each door, opens door number 2 to 9 to reveal dogs. You are now given the option to stick with door 1 or switch to door 10. What is the probability of winning the bike if you do not stick with your initial choice?
questions.explanation: Your initial chosen door has a 1/10 chance of having the bike. The remaining 9 doors collectively have a 9/10 chance of containing the bike. Monty eliminates 8 of these 9 dog's doors, leaving only one unopened door among the original group of nine that you didn't choose initially. This concentrates the entire 9/10 probability onto that single remaining door. Therefore, switching to this last unopened door gives you a 9/10 chance of winning the bike. Hence, the probability of winning the bike if you do not stick with your inital choice is 0.90

questions.explanation: ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699707452aa6d2.93765627.png) Time taken by First Train = 4 hours Time taken by Second Train = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_699707453d2657.86946472.gif) hours Let, total distance between Station A and Station B = LCM of 4 & ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997074557b823.00245131.gif) = 28 unit Speed ratio = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970745722181.12646998.gif) : ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970745add174.74511544.gif) = 7:8 So, distance covered by First train in 2 hours = 7×2 = 14 unit [till 8 am] Now, time taken to cover the remaining distance by both trains and time taken to cross each other = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_69970745c2e3e5.97245938.gif) ×60 = 56 minutes Hence, meeting time = 08:56 am

questions.question: Each one of five men independently can complete a work in 25 days. The work is started by one person. Next day one more person joins and every next day one more person joins. From the fifth day, five persons continued working as a team. In how many days, will the work be completed?
questions.explanation: Let, total work to be completed = 25 unit Thus, efficiency of each man = 1 unit/day Work done in first five days = 1 + 2 + 3 + 4 + 5 = 15 unit So, time taken to complete the remaining work = ![](https://www.thepunditsofficial.in/admin/uploads/questions/q_6997074a15c4e3.71374476.gif) = 2 days Hence, total time taken to complete the whole work = 5 + 2 = 7 days

questions.question: A mixture of acid and water contains 25% acid. When 10 litres of water is added to the mixture, then the percentage of acid becomes 20%. What is the original quantity of the mixture?
questions.explanation: Since, Quantity of Acid is constant in the mixture. Ratio of Initial and Final percentage of acid in the mixture = 25:20 = 5:4 Therefore, total quantity ratio of initial and final mixture = 4:5 110 litres Hence, original quantity of the mixture = 4×10 = 40 litres

questions.question: In public Greek life, a man had to make his way at every step through the immediate persuasion of the spoken word. Whether it be addressing an assembly, a law—court or a more restricted body, his oratory would be a public affair rather than under the purview of a quiet committee, without the support of circulated commentary, and with no backcloth of daily reportage to make his own or others' views familiar to his hearers. The oratory's immediate effect was all—important; it would be naive to expect that mere reasonableness or an inherently good case would equate to a satisfactory appeal. Therefore, it was early realized that persuasion was an art, up to a point teachable, and a variety of specific pedagogy was well established in the second half of the fifth century. When the sophists claimed to teach their pupils how to succeed in public life, rhetoric was a large part of what they meant, though, to do them justice, it was not the whole. The contests of Attic tragedy exhibit all the tricks of this trade, as well as the art of the poets; and the private life of the Greeks was lived so much in public that the pervasive rhetorical manner crept in here too. Skill naturally bred mistrust. If a man of good will had need of expression advanced of mere twaddle, to learn how to expound his contention effectively, the truculent or pugnacious could be taught to dress their case in well—seeming guise. It was a standing charge against the sophists that they ?made the worse appear the better cause,' and it was this immoral lesson which the hero of Aristophanes' Clouds went to learn from, of all people, Socrates. Again, the charge is often made in court that the opponent is an adroit orator and the jury must be circumspect so as not to let him delude them. From the frequency with which this crops up, it is patent that the accusation of cleverness might damage a man. In Greece, juries, of course, were familiar with the style, and would recognize the more evident artifices, but it was worth a litigant's while to get his speech written for him by an expert. Persuasive oratory was certainly one of the pressures that would be effective in an Athenian law—court. A more insidious danger was the inevitable desire to display this art as an art. It is not easy to define the point at which a legitimate concern with style shades off into preoccupation with manner at the expense of matter, but it is easy to perceive that many Greek writers of the fourth and later centuries passed that danger point. The most influential was Isocrates, who polished for long years his pamphlets, written in the form of speeches, and taught to many pupils the smooth and easy periods he had perfected. This was a style of only limited use in the abrupt vicissitudes of politics. Isocrates took to the written word in compensation for his inadequacy in live oratory; the tough and nervous tones of a Demosthenes were far removed from his, though they, too, were based on study and practice. The exaltation of virtuosity did palpable harm. This was not due mainly to the influence of Isocrates: public display was normal and inevitable for a world which talked and listened far more than it read. The balance was always delicate, between style as a vehicle and style as an end in itself.We must not try to pinpoint a specific moment when it, once and for all, tipped over; but certainly, as time went on, virtuosity weighed heavier. While Greek freedom lasted, and it mattered what course of action a Greek city decided to take, rhetoric was a necessary preparation for public life, whatever its side effects. When the study became, in the gloomiest sense of the word, academic, only the side effects remained, and they were not such as to encourage depth of thought. It had been a source of strength for Greek civilization that its problems, of all kinds, were thrashed out very much in public. The shallowness which the study of rhetoric might (not must) encourage was the corresponding weakness. What was a significant consequence of the emphasis on rhetorical skill in ancient Greek public life?
options: It led to a greater emphasis on written communication over oral discourse.
It resulted in a decline in the importance of public speaking in Greek society.
It created a delicate balance between style as a vehicle and style as an end in itself, potentially leading to shallowness.
It eliminated the need for expert speechwriters in Athenian law courts.
questions.explanation: According to the passage, the emphasis on rhetorical skill in ancient Greek public life created a delicate balance between style as a vehicle and style as an end in itself. As time went on, virtuosity weighed heavier, and this led to concerns about shallowness. The passage highlights that while rhetoric was a necessary preparation for public life, its side effects might encourage shallowness.

Identify the type of phrase "the oratory's immediate effect" is in the sentence "The oratory's immediate effect was all-important."
questions.explanation: What is a NOUN PHRASE - A noun phrase is a phrase that consists of a main noun and any accompanying modifiers i.e. adjectives, determiners, etc. that provide more information about the noun. In the sentence "The oratory's immediate effect was all-important," "the oratory's immediate effect" is a noun phrase because it consists of: • A main noun: "effect" • A modifier: "immediate" - adjective • A possessive noun: "the oratory's" - showing ownership or relationship • The phrase "the oratory's immediate effect" functions as a single unit in the sentence, referring to a specific concept or idea. This is a key characteristic of noun phrases. • As the subject of the sentence, the noun phrase "the oratory's immediate effect" is describing the thing that "was all-important."

questions.question: In public Greek life, a man had to make his way at every step through the immediate persuasion of the spoken word. Whether it be addressing an assembly, a law—court or a more restricted body, his oratory would be a public affair rather than under the purview of a quiet committee, without the support of circulated commentary, and with no backcloth of daily reportage to make his own or others' views familiar to his hearers. The oratory's immediate effect was all—important; it would be naive to expect that mere reasonableness or an inherently good case would equate to a satisfactory appeal. Therefore, it was early realized that persuasion was an art, up to a point teachable, and a variety of specific pedagogy was well established in the second half of the fifth century. When the sophists claimed to teach their pupils how to succeed in public life, rhetoric was a large part of what they meant, though, to do them justice, it was not the whole. The contests of Attic tragedy exhibit all the tricks of this trade, as well as the art of the poets; and the private life of the Greeks was lived so much in public that the pervasive rhetorical manner crept in here too. Skill naturally bred mistrust. If a man of good will had need of expression advanced of mere twaddle, to learn how to expound his contention effectively, the truculent or pugnacious could be taught to dress their case in well—seeming guise. It was a standing charge against the sophists that they ?made the worse appear the better cause,' and it was this immoral lesson which the hero of Aristophanes' Clouds went to learn from, of all people, Socrates. Again, the charge is often made in court that the opponent is an adroit orator and the jury must be circumspect so as not to let him delude them. From the frequency with which this crops up, it is patent that the accusation of cleverness might damage a man. In Greece, juries, of course, were familiar with the style, and would recognize the more evident artifices, but it was worth a litigant's while to get his speech written for him by an expert. Persuasive oratory was certainly one of the pressures that would be effective in an Athenian law—court. A more insidious danger was the inevitable desire to display this art as an art. It is not easy to define the point at which a legitimate concern with style shades off into preoccupation with manner at the expense of matter, but it is easy to perceive that many Greek writers of the fourth and later centuries passed that danger point. The most influential was Isocrates, who polished for long years his pamphlets, written in the form of speeches, and taught to many pupils the smooth and easy periods he had perfected. This was a style of only limited use in the abrupt vicissitudes of politics. Isocrates took to the written word in compensation for his inadequacy in live oratory; the tough and nervous tones of a Demosthenes were far removed from his, though they, too, were based on study and practice. The exaltation of virtuosity did palpable harm. This was not due mainly to the influence of Isocrates: public display was normal and inevitable for a world which talked and listened far more than it read. The balance was always delicate, between style as a vehicle and style as an end in itself.We must not try to pinpoint a specific moment when it, once and for all, tipped over; but certainly, as time went on, virtuosity weighed heavier. While Greek freedom lasted, and it mattered what course of action a Greek city decided to take, rhetoric was a necessary preparation for public life, whatever its side effects. When the study became, in the gloomiest sense of the word, academic, only the side effects remained, and they were not such as to encourage depth of thought. It had been a source of strength for Greek civilization that its problems, of all kinds, were thrashed out very much in public. The shallowness which the study of rhetoric might (not must) encourage was the corresponding weakness. Identify the type of clause "whatever its side effects" is in the sentence "While Greek freedom lasted, and it mattered what course of action a Greek city decided to take, rhetoric was a necessary preparation for public life, whatever its side effects."
questions.explanation: The clause "whatever its side effects" is a dependent clause because it cannot stand alone as a complete sentence. It relies on the main clause "rhetoric was a necessary preparation for public life" to make sense. • This clause is an adverbial clause because it modifies the main clause, indicating a concession or a contrast. It suggests that despite the potential side effects, rhetoric was still necessary. • The word "whatever" is a subordinating conjunction that introduces the adverbial clause, indicating a relationship between the two clauses. • Adverbial clauses can indicate various relationships, such as time, place, manner, condition, or concession. In this case, "whatever its side effects" indicates a concession, implying that the side effects are acknowledged but do not change the necessity of rhetoric.

The shallowness which the study of rhetoric might (not must) encourage was the corresponding weakness. What does the word "virtuosity" mean in the context of the passage?
options: A lack of skill or expertise
A tendency to prioritize substance over style
A high level of skill or expertise, especially in a particular art or field
A preference for simplicity and clarity
questions.explanation: In the passage, "virtuosity" refers to the exceptional skill or expertise displayed by some Greek writers and orators. • The passage mentions that "the exaltation of virtuosity did palpable harm," suggesting that an excessive emphasis on showcasing skill or expertise can be detrimental. • The word "virtuosity" typically connotes a sense of mastery, artistry, or technical proficiency, which is consistent with option C. This question requires a good understanding of vocabulary in context, as well as the ability to analyze the passage and determine the intended meaning of the word "virtuosity."

What part of speech is the word "palpable" in the sentence "The exaltation of virtuosity did palpable harm"?
questions.explanation: The word "palpable" is modifying the noun "harm", describing the type or nature of the harm caused by the exaltation of virtuosity. • As a modifier of a noun, "palpable" is functioning as an adjective. • The word "palpable" means able to be felt or perceived, so in this context, it suggests that the harm caused was tangible or noticeable. questions.question: Which of the following sentences contains a spelling error ?
options: In an effort to improve transparency and accountability, the organization introduced a new policy that requires employees to submit detailed reports on all external communications with stakeholders.

After the completion of the environmental impact assessment, the construction company was granted permission to proceed with the project under strict regulatory guidelines set by the local authorities.
The panel of international experts concluded that immediate reforms in the education sector are essential to bridge the gap between urban and rural learning outcomes across the country.
To celebrate the foundation day of the institute, a series of seminars and workshops were organized, featuring prominant scholars and industry leaders from various fields of research.
questions.explanation: The incorrect spelling is - Prominant Correct spelling is - Prominent

questions.question: Select the most appropriate synonym of the underlined word in the following sentence – The young entrepreneur's innovative approach to sustainable energy solutions garnered significant attention and respect from her peers in the industry, who were equally impressed by her passion for environmental stewardship and her ability to effectively collaborate with a diverse range of stakeholders, including government officials and investors.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Associate Let’s see the meaning of all words: Peer - समकक्ष, बराबरी का व्यक्ति - A person of equal status, rank, or ability. Example - In school, students often socialize with their peers during recess. Associate - सहयोगी - A person who works or associates with others Example: The associates in the company worked together on the project. Fickle - अस्थिर - Changing one's mind quickly Example: She's so fickle, always changing her plans. Mingy - कंजूस - Unwilling to give or share Example: The mingy neighbour never contributed to charity. Inferior - हीन - Lower in status or quality Example: The product was considered inferior due to its poor quality.

questions.question: Change the following into indirect speech: He said, “If I had known about the deadline, I would have submitted the report on time.”
options: He said that if he knew about the deadline, he would submit the report on time.
He said that if he had known about the deadline, he would have submitted the report on time.
He said that had he known about the deadline, he would have submitted the report on time.
He said that if he had known about the deadline, he would submit the report on time.
questions.explanation: The sentence is in 3rd conditional and uses past perfect + would have V3 In indirect speech, the tense of the conditional remains the same (unchanged structure). A and D are grammatically incorrect (tense mismatch) C seems tempting but breaks reported speech structure by omitting "if" B retains both grammatical correctness and reporting structure.

questions.question: Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph: S1. Urbanization has rapidly altered the social and environmental landscape of developing nations. A. While it has brought infrastructure and job opportunities, it has also strained resources like water and housing. B. Unplanned growth often leads to congestion, pollution, and inequality in access to basic services. C. Experts emphasize the importance of smart city planning and sustainable urban development. D. Governments are now facing pressure to strike a balance between growth and livability. S6. Without thoughtful planning, the very benefits of urbanization may turn into burdens.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • S1 introduces the impact of urbanization • A gives pros and cons • B expands on problems of unplanned growth • C offers expert recommendations • D shows governments reacting to these issues • S6 closes with a warning→ Logical flow: S1 – A – B – C – D – S6

questions.question: Select the most appropriate antonym of the given underlined word. The ancient temple, with its intricate carvings and majestic architecture, stood as a testament to the imperishable legacy of the long-lost civilization that built it, its grandeur and beauty remaining impervious to the passage of time.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Ephemeral Let’s see the meaning of all words: Imperishable - अविनाशी - Lasting forever, indestructible Example: The imperishable legacy of the great leader continues to inspire people. Eternal - शाश्वत - Lasting forever Example: The eternal beauty of nature is a source of inspiration. Perdurable - स्थायी - Long-lasting Example: The perdurable impact of the discovery was felt for centuries. Ephemeral - अल्पकालिक - Lasting for a very short time Example: The firefly's glow is ephemeral, lasting only for a few seconds. Hock - गिरवी रखना - unrelated to the meaning of imperishable Example: She had to hock her jewellery to pay the bills.

questions.question: Choose the most appropriate option to make the sentence sensibly correct. The firefly's glow was ________, lasting only for a brief moment before it vanished in the darkness, leaving behind a memory of its mesmerizing sparkle.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - Fleeting Let’s see the meaning of all words: Sparing - मितव्ययी - Characterized by restraint in expenditure or use of resources Example: She's sparing with her words, always concise. Fleeting - क्षणिक - Passing quickly; brief in duration Example: The fleeting glimpse of the sunset was breathtaking. Fury - क्रोध - Intense, uncontrolled anger Example: The storm raged with fury, causing destruction. Voluble - वाचाल - Speaking or characterized by a flowing, abundant, or excessive quality of speech Example: She's a voluble person, always chatting with friends.

questions.question: What does the idiom "gild the lily" mean in the following sentence? "Adding a fancy introduction to the already excellent report would be gilding the lily, and might even detract from its impact."
options: To improve something that is already perfect
To criticize something that is already flawed
To add unnecessary embellishments to something that is already good enough
To simplify something that is overly complex
questions.explanation: Meaning: The idiom "gild the lily" means to add unnecessary embellishments or decorations to something that is already good enough or even excellent. It implies that the additional effort or ornamentation might not only be superfluous but also potentially detrimental, as it could detract from the original's simplicity and effectiveness. Origin: The phrase is derived from Shakespeare's play "King John," where it is said, "To gild refined gold, to paint the lily... is wasteful and ridiculous excess." Gilding refers to the process of applying a thin layer of gold to a surface, while painting the lily would be adding color to something that is already perfectly beautiful in its natural state. In Context: In the sentence provided earlier, "Adding a fancy introduction to the already excellent report would be gilding the lily, and might even detract from its impact," the speaker is suggesting that the report is already well-written and effective. Adding a fancy introduction might be seen as unnecessary and could potentially make the report less effective, rather than enhancing it.

questions.question: Choose the correct passive form of the sentence below: “Someone might have been leaking confidential documents to the media for months.”
options: Confidential documents might be leaked to the media by someone for months.
Confidential documents might have leaked to the media for months.
Confidential documents might have been being leaked to the media by someone for months.
Confidential documents were being leaked to the media for months by someone.
questions.explanation: • Tense: Modal + Perfect Continuous ("might have been leaking") • Passive: might have been being + V3 • Tricky part: Most people ignore “being” after “been” → "been being leaked" • Hence, correct transformation: “Confidential documents might have been being leaked…”

questions.question: What single word can replace the phrase "showing characteristics of being weak, feeble, or excessively concerned with trivial matters, often in a way that is perceived as unsuitable for someone of a certain age or position"?
questions.explanation: Let's break down each option: A) Meticulous: - Means extremely careful and precise, paying great attention to details. - Describes someone who is thorough and fastidious in their work or activities. B) Anile: - Means weak, feeble, or excessively concerned with trivial matters, often in a way that's perceived as unsuitable for someone of a certain age or position. - Typically used to describe someone, often an older person, who has become frail or overly focused on insignificant things. C) Halcyon:- Typically refers to a period of time in the past that was idyllically happy and peaceful. It can also describe a state of calmness, serenity, or tranquility. D) Infirm: - Means weak or frail, often due to illness, age, or physical condition. - Describes someone who is physically or mentally weak, and may require assistance or care. Infirm vs Anile - Infirm typically means weak or frail, often due to illness, age, or physical condition. It can describe someone who is physically or mentally weak, and may be unable to perform daily activities without assistance. In comparison to "anile", which connotes a sense of weakness or feebleness often accompanied by excessive concern with trivial matters, "infirm" tends to focus more on physical or mental weakness without necessarily implying a specific behavior or attitude.

questions.question: Convert the following sentence into indirect speech: The CEO said, “Had we anticipated the market crash in advance, we would have diversified our investments and avoided significant losses.”
options: The CEO said that if they would have anticipated the market crash in advance, they would diversify their investments and avoid significant losses.
The CEO said that they had anticipated the market crash in advance and that’s why they diversified their investments and avoided significant losses.
The CEO said that had they anticipated the market crash in advance, they would have diversified their investments and avoided significant losses.
The CEO said that if they had anticipated the market crash in advance, they would diversify their investments and avoided significant losses.
questions.explanation: Explanation: 1. Type of Sentence: This is a third conditional sentence, used to express unreal past conditions → "Had we anticipated... we would have..." 2. Direct to Indirect Speech Rules Applied: No change in tense is required inside conditional sentences when converting to indirect speech if the condition is already in the past perfect form (e.g., "Had we anticipated"). "We" changes to "they" (pronoun shift). The sentence starts with "The CEO said that..." — the rest of the sentence remains structurally intact as it's already indirect in tone.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate meaning of the given idiom. The young entrepreneur's innovative startup idea was initially hailed as a game-changer, but it ultimately proved to be a flash in the pan, failing to sustain its early momentum and fading into obscurity as more established companies continued to dominate the market.
options: An early promise that fails to materialize
questions.explanation: The correct answer is - An early promise that fails to materialize Flash in the pan - क्षणिक सफलता - A brief, fleeting success or popularity that doesn't last Example - The musician was considered a flash in the pan after his debut single topped the charts, but he was unable to produce another hit song, and his fame faded quickly.

questions.question: What single word can replace the phrase: "A person who engages in public discourse or political debate not to seek truth or justice, but to stir controversy, provoke emotional reactions, and disrupt rational discussion, often through inflammatory or deceptive statements"?
questions.explanation: • Demagogue - • A political figure who gains support by exploiting emotions, fears, and prejudices. Often uses deceptive or inflammatory rhetoric, not truth or justice. • Best fit for the full description in the question. • Polemicist - • A person who argues forcefully or aggressively, especially in writing. • Focus is on argumentation, not necessarily manipulation or emotional provocation. • Close, but lacks the element of public deception or emotional exploitation. • Sophist - • Someone who uses clever but fallacious arguments, often for deception. • Close in meaning, especially the deceptive part, but not always political or public-facing. • Historically associated with misleading philosophical reasoning, not necessarily inciting controversy. • Populist - • Someone who appeals to the interests of the general population, especially against the elite. • May use emotion but not always in a malicious or disruptive way. • Lacks the intent to provoke or deceive as described in the question.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.
options: on a road trip, enjoying the
questions.explanation: The error lies in segment: Me and my friend Explanation: • The phrase "Me and my friend" is incorrect because "me" is an object pronoun and should not be used as the subject of a sentence. • The correct subject pronoun to use is "I". • Also, according to the 231 rule in English grammar, when using multiple pronouns together, the order should be: • 2nd person [you], 3rd person [he/she/they], and then 1st person [I] — that is: You, He/She, and I. • However, when including another person by name [like "my friend"], the name usually comes first, followed by "I". • So, instead of "Me and my friend", the correct form is "My friend and I". Corrected Sentence: "My friend and I were on a road trip, enjoying the music and the open road."

questions.question: Convert the following sentence into passive voice: “The audit committee, after reviewing multiple discrepancies in the quarterly financial statements submitted by various departments, directed the finance head to prepare a comprehensive report highlighting all irregularities and submit it to the board by Friday.”
options: A comprehensive report highlighting all irregularities was directed to be prepared by the audit committee and submitted to the board by Friday by the finance head after multiple discrepancies in the quarterly financial statements were reviewed.
After reviewing multiple discrepancies in the financial statements, the audit committee asked the finance head that he must prepare a report and submit it to the board by Friday.
The finance head was asked by the audit committee to prepare a comprehensive report of the irregularities and to send it to the board after reviewing financial statements.
The audit committee was reviewing discrepancies and asked the finance head to prepare a report and submit it to the board by Friday.
questions.explanation: Let’s break it down: • Main verb in active: "directed the finance head to prepare a comprehensive report and submit it…" • Passive structure: • “Directed someone to do something” → “Something was directed to be done by someone” • Complexity added by: participial phrase (“after reviewing…”), and double action (“prepare and submit”) • Option A correctly converts both actions (“prepare” and “submit”) into passive and preserves the correct agent (audit committee) and receiver (finance head). • B, C, D are either semi-active, tense-shifted, or drop key passive construction, hence incorrect.

questions.question: S1. The spread of misinformation during the COVID-19 pandemic posed a serious threat to public health. A. It ranged from fake remedies circulating on social media to conspiracy theories about vaccines. B. The World Health Organization even coined the term "infodemic" to describe this overabundance of information. C. Governments and platforms struggled to contain this parallel crisis that amplified fear and confusion. D. Experts warned that misinformation could undermine the effectiveness of the global response. S6. Addressing this challenge required coordinated efforts involving fact-checkers, media platforms, and public education campaigns.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • S1 sets the context: "misinformation during COVID" • A directly describes what kind of misinformation → fake remedies, conspiracy theories • D follows logically, as experts give warnings about its effects • B introduces the term “infodemic” from WHO, elaborating on seriousness • C then talks about how authorities tried to contain it • S6 concludes with the solution: joint efforts→ Correct sequence: S1 – A – D – B – C – S6

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. Can you be able to please give the Hindi meaning of each word.
options: Can you please be able to give
Are you be able to please give
questions.explanation: Correct Answer: Can you please give Explanation: • The original phrase "Can you be able to" is grammatically incorrect and redundant, because "can" and "be able to" both express ability — using both together is unnecessary. • The polite and grammatically correct form is: "Can you please give..." • This keeps the sentence simple, clear, and correct. Here are the options explained: • [A] Can you be able to giving – Incorrect; the phrase "be able to giving" is grammatically wrong. It should be "be able to give". • [B] Can you please give – Correct; this is a polite and grammatically accurate way to ask someone to do something. • [C] Are you be able to please give – Incorrect; mixes "are" with "be", which is not a valid structure. Final Correct Sentence: "Can you please give the Hindi meaning of each word?"

questions.question: Select the Grammatically Incorrect sentence -
options: Alms are given to the poor by kind-hearted people.
I saw sands on the beach that stretched along the shore.
Sheep provide wool for making clothes.
The general praised the infantry for their courage and discipline.
questions.explanation: Incorrect Sentence: I saw sands on the beach that stretched along the shore. Why it's wrong: The word "sands" is incorrect here because "sand" is an uncountable noun in general usage. Word Meaning Usage Example Sand Refers to small, loose grains of rock or minerals [uncountable noun]. Used when referring to sand in general or in bulk. "The sand on the beach is warm." Sands Plural form, used in poetic or literary contexts or to describe different types of sand. Often used figuratively or symbolically. "The sands of time slip away." Corrected version: "I saw sand on the beach that stretched along the shore."

questions.question: Select the option that will improve the underlined part of the given sentence. In case no improvement is needed, select 'No improvement required'. When things got tough, he had to Fall at his savings to pay for the emergency.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is: Fall back on Explanation: The phrasal verb "fall back on" is the most appropriate choice here. It means to rely on something for support or assistance in times of difficulty, which fits perfectly in this sentence, as it talks about relying on savings during an emergency. Here are the meanings and examples of all the phrasal verbs: • Fall back on – किसी पर निर्भर होना – To rely on something when other options are unavailable. Example: "When his business failed, he had to fall back on his savings to survive." • Fall out – झगड़ना – To have an argument or disagreement, often leading to the end of a relationship or friendship. Example: "They fell out after a heated argument." • Fall through – नाकाम होना – To fail or not happen as planned. Example: "Their plans for a vacation fell through because of the weather." • Fall at – Incorrect/Not a valid phrasal verb in this context Corrected Sentence: "When things got tough, he had to fall back on his savings to pay for the emergency."

questions.question: Rearrange the sentences A, B, C, D to form a meaningful paragraph between the fixed introductory sentence (S1) and the concluding sentence (S6). S1. In recent years, the concept of remote work has evolved from a temporary solution to a transformative shift in the global work culture. A. Many organizations initially adopted remote work out of necessity during the pandemic, but gradually discovered unexpected benefits like increased productivity and reduced operational costs. B. However, this new way of working is not without its challenges, as employees often report issues like digital fatigue, blurred boundaries between work and personal life, and a sense of isolation. C. Experts argue that the future of work lies in a hybrid model, where organizations blend the flexibility of remote work with the structure and collaboration of traditional office spaces. D. As companies reimagine their workplace strategies, there is a growing emphasis on redefining employee engagement, mental well-being, and digital infrastructure to support long-term adaptability. S6. Clearly, the remote work revolution demands not just technological readiness, but also a fundamental shift in how we perceive productivity, teamwork, and workplace culture.
questions.explanation: Let’s walk through the logic: S1 introduces the shift from temporary to permanent remote work culture. A should come first: • It provides background: how remote work started during the pandemic and evolved with benefits. • It directly continues the timeline introduced in S1. B logically follows: • Introduces challenges that came with the benefits in A. • The word “However” shows contrast, and the issues mentioned directly stem from A’s context. D follows B: • Now that problems are presented, D offers the response from companies: focus on strategies and employee support. • It also signals a transition from challenges to solutions. C logically comes last: • It presents the expert opinion and vision for the future: hybrid work model. • It sets up S6, which talks about broader cultural shifts.

questions.question: Choose the best passive form: “Had they not informed the authorities on time, the situation could have escalated further.”
options: Had the authorities not been informed on time by them, the situation could have escalated further.
The authorities were not informed on time, which could have escalated the situation.
If they hadn't informed the authorities on time, the situation could be escalated further.
If the authorities had been informed later, the situation would have escalated.
questions.explanation: Explanation: • Conditional form using past perfect • Passive structure: had + been + V3 • “Had they not informed…” → “Had the authorities not been informed…” • A is grammatically accurate and matches both the conditional form and passive structure • B, C, D either change the meaning or the tense.
